description:
Add a pre-commit hook that verifies Jest/Vitest configs properly exclude buck-out directories.

The check should:
1. Detect if project uses Jest (jest.config.js, jest.config.ts, or jest field in package.json)
2. Detect if project uses Vitest (vitest.config.js, vitest.config.ts)
3. Verify the config includes exclusion patterns for buck-out and/or dot directories:
   - Jest: testPathIgnorePatterns should include '/buck-out/' or '/\\.'
   - Vitest: exclude should include '**/buck-out/**' or '**/.*/**'
4. Warn (or fail) if JS/TS tests are present but no exclusion is configured

This prevents spurious test failures from buck-out artifacts being picked up by test discovery.

Implementation options:
- Shell script in pre-commit
- Python script for more robust config parsing
- Could be part of existing turnkey-check or a new js-test-config-check

Related: turnkey-zk3j (documentation task)
title: Pre-commit check for JS/TS test config buck-out exclusion
